results.text:
Evaluating RAG systems requires measuring both retrieval quality and generation faithfulness separately, as failures in either stage lead to poor overall performance.

For retrieval evaluation, precision@k measures the fraction of retrieved documents in the top k that are relevant. Recall@k measures the fraction of all relevant documents retrieved in the top k. Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) measures the average inverse rank of the first relevant document. Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (nDCG) accounts f

laim in the generated answer is supported by the retrieved context.

For hallucination detection, compare the generated answer against retrieved context to identify claims made without supporting evidence. BERTScore measures semantic similarity between generated and reference answers. End-to-end latency must be decomposed into embedding latency, vector search latency, and generation latency to identify bottlenecks. Typical targets are retrieval under 100ms and generation under 10 seconds.

inverse rank of the first relevant document. Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (nDCG) accounts for graded relevance and position.

For generation evaluation, RAGAS is a framework specifically designed for RAG evaluation with metrics including faithfulness (is the answer supported by retrieved context?), answer relevance, context precision, and context recall. Faithfulness is computed by checking if each claim in the generated answer is supported by the retrieved context.

steps.input_preview: Evaluating RAG systems requires measuring both retrieval quality and generation faithfulness separately, as failures in …

output.summary: To evaluate Retrieval-and-Generation (RAG) systems, both retrieval quality and generation faithfulness need assessment for optimal performance. For retrieval evaluation, precision@k, recall@k, Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR), and Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (nDCG) are used to assess the accuracy and relevance of the retrieved documents. BERTScore is employed for generation evaluation to measure semantic similarity between generated answers and reference texts. For hallucination detection, the generated answer is compared against the retrieved context to identify unsubstantiated claims. RAGAS, a specific framework for RAG evaluation, assesses faithfulness, answer relevance, context precision, and context recall in the generated answers.

steps.output_preview: RAG (Retrieval-and-Generation) is an system that combines both retrieval and generation of information to provide accurate and relevant responses. By measuring the precision and recall of retrieved do…

final_answer: RAG (Retrieval-and-Generation) is an system that combines both retrieval and generation of information to provide accurate and relevant responses. By measuring the precision and recall of retrieved documents, Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR), Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (nDCG), and other metrics, RAG ensures the quality of the retrieved data. For the generated answers, BERTScore is used to measure semantic similarity with reference texts, and a framework called RAGAS assesses faithfulness, answer relevance, context precision, and context recall to reduce hallucinations (unsubstantiated claims).
